title: Request Authority, Host, Target Reconstruction, and Routing · Language Lens: Host Field Requirement
summary: A server trusts any Host, accepts duplicate authority sources, reconstructs links from forwarded input, routes before validating the target, and confuses TLS identity with request authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the host field requirement increment in authority validation and target reconstruction table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.
Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Host Field Requirement · Theory Host Field Requirement
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2405 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive and allowlist one effective request authority without trusting disagreement; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Host Field Requirement contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Host Field Requirement · Theory Host Field Requirement.

For Predict Host Field Requirement · Theory Host Field Requirement, keep Host Field Requirement connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
